entry.title: Beyond the Data Silo: The Convergence of SAP Systems and Modern Data Lake Technologies
entry.content:
In today's enterprise landscape, a fascinating transformation is underway. While SAP systems continue to serve as the backbone of business operations, organizations are discovering that traditional approaches to data management no longer meet the demands of our rapidly evolving digital economy. The challenge isn't just about managing data – it's about turning it into actionable intelligence at the speed of business.

The Evolution of SAP Data Architecture

The SAP ecosystem has long been the backbone of enterprise operations, housing critical business data across its various modules. However, today's digital economy demands more than just robust transaction processing – it requires seamless integration of internal and external data sources, real-time analytics, and AI-driven insights.

The current state of enterprise data presents an intriguing paradox. Consider a typical global manufacturing Company X: Multiple SAP instances span continents, each running critical modules like FI/CO, MM, SD, and PP. In Europe, teams work with one set of customizations and configurations, while their Americas counterparts operate with different chart of accounts and business rules. What seems like a simple question about global inventory levels can spiral into a days-long exercise involving multiple teams, countless Excel sheets, and lengthy email threads.

 

Traditional-architecture

These challenges extend beyond just SAP systems. The integration of external data sources – IoT sensors, market data feeds, social media analytics – adds layers of complexity to an already intricate landscape. The time lag between data creation and insight generation has become a critical bottleneck for business agility.

The Promise of Modern Data Lakes

Modern data lake technologies represent a paradigm shift in how enterprises can manage and utilize their data. These platforms bring transformative capabilities through their support for industry-standard formats, enabling seamless data exchange across the enterprise. Their flexible schema management adapts to changing business needs, while enterprise-grade reliability is ensured through ACID transactions. The ability to access and restore historical data states, combined with unified processing for both batch and streaming workloads, creates a robust foundation for next-generation enterprise data management.

 

The Convergence: A New Enterprise Data Architecture

The future lies in architectures that bring together the best of both worlds – SAP's robust business processes and the flexibility of modern data lakes. This next-generation architecture reimagines how enterprise data flows and interacts:

 

Future-architecture

 This convergence enables a new approach to data governance, where metadata management, data quality rules, and lineage tracking are centralized across both SAP and non-SAP data. Organizations can achieve zero-latency access to operational data with instant synchronization across systems, while performing real-time analytics without impacting transaction systems.

Perhaps most importantly, this convergence creates an AI-ready data foundation. By standardizing data formats for AI/ML workloads and enabling the integration of structured and unstructured data, organizations can fully leverage the power of large language models and generative AI across their enterprise data landscape.

Practical Implementation Strategies

Organizations looking to modernize their SAP data architecture should begin with a focus on high-value scenarios that require integrated data, particularly in areas where real-time insights drive business value. Projects that combine SAP and external data often yield the most significant returns and provide valuable learning experiences for teams. The foundation of this modernization requires implementing modern data lake capabilities alongside existing systems, while establishing unified governance frameworks. Creating semantic layers that abstract technical complexity enables broader adoption across the organization. Organizations must also prepare their data structures for AI workloads, implementing robust data quality measures and building pipelines for continuous data refreshes. 

Conclusion: Preparing for Tomorrow

The convergence of SAP systems with modern data lake technologies isn't just a technical evolution – it's a business imperative. Organizations that successfully navigate this transformation will find themselves better positioned to accelerate innovation through integrated data access, improve decision-making with real-time insights, enable AI-driven business processes, and maintain competitive advantage in the digital economy.

The question isn't whether to embrace this transformation, but how to implement it in a way that maximizes business value while minimizing disruption. As technology leaders, our role is to guide our organizations through this evolution, ensuring we build data architectures that are not just modern, but future-ready.

What steps is your organization taking to modernize its enterprise data architecture? The journey toward unified, AI-ready data platforms is just beginning, and the decisions we make today will shape our ability to compete tomorrow.

entry.title: Announcing the Water Track at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025
entry.content:
For the third consecutive year, we are happy to announce a dedicated track for water and wastewater companies in the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference agenda for this year.

It will take place on Wednesday afternoon April 9th with the aim to recognize the success of this section in previous conference editions and continue giving floor to a selection of exceptional professionals not only presenting, but building relationships and developing partnerships among this industry group.

Shifting from an agenda designed last year to show the concept architecture built by SAP to support water management processes to help organizations leveraging their software investments to tackle processes such as smart meter data management, named the “Smart Platform for Water”, current year be focused on cases where SAP portfolio of applications and technologies can help organizations confront their challenges and obtain operational benefits.

Mrs Inge Opreel, Farys CIO

I am delighted to inform you about the presentions of the Water Track:

  • SAP for Water and Wastewater Services: Building the Resilient Water Company (SAP Keynote)
  • ESRI ArcGIS on HANA: Building Next Generation of Integrated Asset Management at FARYS
  • Streaming Service Operations: SAP Field Service Management (FSM) Integration at Vitens

But the agenda of water-specific items will not be restricted to this track but will be highly recommended water-specific or water-relevant conferences before and after the Water Track which will configure an entire agenda specifically for delegates of this industry:

SAP Business Data Cloud

Wednesday morning, April 9th (before the Water Track): suggested slots unveil the large ongoing updates in the portfolio of Asset Management, including an interesting use case of Asset Performance Management as well as an excellent presentation by SAP Product Development of the evolution of SAP analytical  portfolio, this is Business Data Cloud:   

  • Latest Innovations in SAP Asset Management (Asset Management track)
  • Business Data Cloud – Unlocking the Power of Unified Data
  • SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) at AkerBP:  From Test Environment to Production

Thursday morning, April 10th will be a continuation of the water-specific cases, presenting the application of AI with SAP technology platform at FARYS, a live demonstration at the Pop-up Campus (which will require a pre-booking)

 

 

 

Inside of SAP Pop-Up Campus

A very interesting use case of RISE conversion, from a perspective very close to the reality of many water companies'.

  • Unlocking the Potential of AI with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Datasphere (SAP Microforum at 10:30AM 
  • A Day in a Life of a Water Drop: Deep Dive into the Benefits of Asset Management at the Lifecycle of a Waterdrop (session needs to be pre-booked via the app)
  • Transformation from OnPrem to SAP RISE: Lessons from Andel’s Journey (AI Track)

Overall, the complete conference is designed for an era based in networks, relationships and reputation, where collaboration and developing partnerships is the key of success. But it is known that such relationships cannot be automated and the soul of this track, and the overall water community formation, is the inheritance of the Executive Value Network for Water and Waste Water Companies workshops. This is a regular meeting of water companies taking place in Europe (already six editions), Latin America (three) and now Africa. Participants highlight common challenges and go along with case studies of how SAP customers are leveraging SAP solutions to address them. Take this unique opportunity to connect with your peers, gain insights into industry trends, and chart your development plan with the latest technologies of 2025!

 Looking forward to seeing you in Rotterdam!

entry.title: AI@FRE (Part 5) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI for People and Team
entry.content:
### AI@FRE Blog Series:

AI@FRE (Part 1) - Enabling Enterprise AI Adoption through the BTP FRE Engagement Model
AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework
AI@FRE (Part 3) – Guardrails for Enterprise AI Adoption
AI@FRE (Part 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails
AI@FRE (Part 5) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI for People and Team
AI@FRE (PART 6) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – Establish AI Ethics, Security & Privacy

###

Adopting AI in the enterprise goes beyond implementing technology; it requires a people-centered approach. Organizations that successfully integrate AI empower their teams with new skills, redefine roles, and foster a collaborative environment where AI becomes a strategic enabler rather than a disruptor. This blog explores two essential steps for AI adoption: embracing AI within the organization and building robust, AI-capable teams. 

Embracing and Onboarding AI in the Workplace 

For AI to become part of the enterprise fabric, employees need to feel supported, informed, and engaged. Moving AI from a novelty to a standardized tool requires careful alignment with organizational goals and a focus on continuous learning. 

Define the Challenge 

Many employees are apprehensive about AI, often viewing it as complex or a potential threat to job security. Overcoming these perceptions involves: 

  • Effective Communication and Training: Employees must understand how AI can assist rather than replace them. 
  • Empowering Employees: Provide tools and support to integrate AI into everyday tasks and processes. 
  • Cultivating a Culture of Innovation: Encourage experimentation, learning from mistakes, and collaborative exploration of AI’s potential across roles. 

Approach 

  • Communicate and Align: Clearly communicate AI’s capabilities and align them with organizational goals. This ensures employees understand how AI aligns with the company's vision and how it will enhance their roles. 
  • Identify Use Cases: Identify specific business challenges where AI can make a difference, such as automating repetitive tasks or enabling faster, data-driven decisions. 
  • Pilot Projects: Start small by implementing pilot projects that demonstrate AI’s impact on real tasks. These early successes help build confidence and support for further AI initiatives. 
  • Governance and Ethics: Develop a framework to ensure AI is used responsibly. Considerations around data privacy, fairness, and ethical use are paramount to building trust. 
  • Continuous Improvement: Learn from each AI project to mature AI adoption. Celebrate successes, iterate on learnings, and keep refining AI implementations for improved performance and adoption. 

Outcomes and Follow-Up Actions 

  • Invest in Training and Change Management: Ongoing training is critical to help employees gain new skills and fully understand the role of AI in their jobs. 
  • Augment Human Skills: Use AI to optimize and automate repetitive, tiring tasks, allowing employees to focus on more creative, strategic responsibilities. 
  • Opportunity to Redefine Jobs: With AI handling routine tasks, employees can redefine their roles to work alongside AI, focusing on higher-level problem-solving and innovative projects. 

Building AI Teams, Enhancing Capabilities, and Complementing Skill Sets 

Building a capable AI team requires hiring talent, upskilling existing employees, and integrating AI expertise across departments. By assembling an AI-proficient team, organizations create the foundation needed for strategic AI deployment and continuous improvement. 

Define the Challenge 

Organizations face challenges when trying to integrate AI into existing workflows and business processes. A successful AI team should: 

  • Address Skill Gaps: Existing teams may lack AI expertise, necessitating new hires or targeted training. 
  • Facilitate Seamless AI Integration: AI should align smoothly with various business functions, avoiding disruptions. 
  • Foster a Collaborative Environment: Effective AI adoption requires cross-functional cooperation to leverage AI’s full potential. 

Approach 

  • Talent Acquisition: Recruit professionals with expertise in AI, machine learning, data science, and related fields. Skilled hires strengthen the core AI team, bringing fresh perspectives and knowledge. 
  • Training and Development: Offer training programs for existing employees to enhance AI skills, complementing their current skill sets and fostering adaptability. 
  • Cross-functional Collaboration: Promote collaboration between AI experts and other departments. By pairing domain experts with AI specialists, organizations can drive innovation that’s informed by practical, hands-on knowledge. 
  • Continuous Learning: Encourage a culture of ongoing learning to keep the team updated on AI advancements, emerging tools, and best practices. 
  • Trusted Partnerships and External Collaborations: Establish partnerships with institutions, industry experts, and other AI-specialized organizations to supplement internal expertise, bringing in specialized insights and resources. 

 

Outcomes and Follow-Up Actions 

  • Define an AI Strategy: A proficient team can actively shape the organization’s AI strategy and uphold AI ethics, ensuring responsible AI usage. 
  • Train, Explain, and Sustain AI: The team should be skilled at training AI systems, explaining outcomes to stakeholders, and ensuring continuous monitoring for proper functionality. 
  • Augment AI Skills for Scalability: As the organization scales AI, standardized processes and tools become vital, and an empowered AI team will be prepared to lead these efforts, supporting the wider organization in adopting AI more systematically. 

 

Building a Sustainable AI Culture for the Future 

Creating a sustainable AI culture is an ongoing journey. It begins by fostering AI adoption at the individual and team levels, equipping employees with the tools and training they need to integrate AI into daily operations. AI-powered organizations focus on: 

  1. Empowering Teams: Use AI to relieve employees of repetitive tasks, enabling them to focus on impactful, creative work. 
  1. Continuous Skill Enhancement: Organizations should prioritize training and development to stay at the forefront of AI innovation. 
  1. Purpose-Driven AI Projects: AI should support clear, purpose-driven goals, demonstrating tangible business value and improving organizational agility. 

 

An organization with AI-empowered employees and cross-functional AI teams is better equipped to adapt to market changes, make data-driven decisions, and continuously innovate. By investing in people-first AI adoption, businesses can not only enhance operational efficiency but also create an environment where AI is a valued, collaborative tool for every team member. 

 

Curious on a structured customer engagement model to set up standardized Cloud Architecture for Business AI Adoption? Connect with our Global BTP Customer Engagement Team at BTPFRE@sap.com.

entry.title: Leveraging API Traffic Predictions in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced digital landscape, efficiently managing API traffic and predicting future trends are crucial for maintaining seamless operations. SAP Integration Suite introduces a powerful feature called Predictions, which leverages AI to forecast future API call volumes based on historical data. This blog post will guide you through understanding, enabling, and viewing predictions, helping you optimize your API strategy and make informed decisions.

 

Key Use Cases

Traffic Predictions:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the API traffic prediction for all your deployed APIs for any duration in the next three months. They can further be aggregated to see the data day-wise, week-wise or month-wise.
    • Forecasting the API traffic during a specific time period enables planning ahead on resource allocation, optimizing costs, improving performance, reliability, and scalability. For example, a travel booking site might predict increased API traffic during holiday seasons. This foresight allows the site to scale up its infrastructure in advance, ensuring it can handle the higher demand without performance issues.

Weekly Trends:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the typical weekly trend of API traffic in your tenant.
    • Weekly trends help identify API traffic patterns that occur within a week, aiding decision-making. For example, an e-commerce platform might notice higher API traffic on weekends due to increased shopping activity. This insight allows the platform to allocate more resources during weekends to handle the surge in traffic and ensure a smooth user experience.

Daily Trends:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the typical daily trend of API traffic in your tenant.
    • Daily trends help identify API traffic patterns during a 24-hour period and enable decision-making. For instance, a food delivery service might observe peak API traffic during lunch and dinner hours. By identifying these peak hours, the service can avoid rolling out updates during these times to minimize disruption and ensure timely deliveries.

 

How does it work?  

The Predictions feature in SAP Integration Suite analyzes past API call data to forecast future traffic volumes. By identifying trends, it provides insights into expected traffic patterns, helping API owners make informed decisions.

 

Enabling Predictions

As an Administrator of a specific Integration Suite - API Management tenant, one could decide to turn the feature on from the Settings -> APIs page of the Integration Suite application with a simple toggle switch. The toggle button activates both Anomaly Detection and Predictions for API calls.

Note: The feature is in the process of getting updated in our global Data centres. Check this Note for information about regional availability.

Note: The availability of the feature is dependent on your SAP Integration Suite service plan. Check this Note for information about the various plans and supported features.

 

Viewing Predictions and Traffic Trends

Once enabled, you can view API call predictions and trends in the analytics dashboard under a separate tab called Predictions. It offers a visualization of expected traffic patterns over a specified timeframe. You can customize the duration and frequency of predictions to suit your needs.

 

Key Features

  • API Call Predictions: Analyze future API call traffic for specific timeframes such as daily, weekly, or monthly.
  • API Call Trends: Examine the frequency of API calls within set timeframes, such as hourly or daily.
  • Customizable Options: Select specific API proxies, choose the prediction duration, and set the frequency according to your preferences.

 

Benefits of Using Predictions

By leveraging the Predictions feature, you can:

  • Optimize Resource Allocation: Ensure your resources are allocated efficiently based on expected traffic.
  • Manage System Load: Anticipate high traffic periods and prepare your systems accordingly.
  • Make Informed Decisions: Use insights from traffic trends to refine your API strategy and improve overall performance.

 

Conclusion

The Predictions feature in SAP Integration Suite is a valuable tool for any organization looking to enhance its API management capabilities. By forecasting future API call volumes and identifying traffic trends, you can optimize resource allocation, manage system load, and make informed decisions to drive your business forward.

As always, we look forward to hearing your feedback!

entry.title: AI-assisted process modeling: instantly transform text into BPMN diagrams
entry.content:
Transforming the future of business process management 

The landscape of process modeling is evolving rapidly. As the demand for documenting and analyzing business processes increases, process modelers face growing challenges in keeping up with the pace. Limited capacity, uncertainty on where to start, and the need for more efficient collaboration between process experts and BPMN specialists often slow down the process. 

What if you could begin with a draft and refine it instead of starting from scratch? What if business experts, rather than only BPMN specialists, could contribute directly to process models? These are the questions driving SAP Signavio’s upcoming innovation: AI-assisted process modeler, text to process (see availability and activation).  

Introducing AI-assisted process modeler, text to process in SAP Signavio 

As the first feature under our AI-assisted process modeler initiative, text to process enables process modelers and subject matter experts to significantly reduce the time required to create new processes. By simply providing a text prompt, AI automatically translates it into a well-structured BPMN diagram, which can then be further refined and customized. 

Check it out with this video.

How it works 

Imagine you have just concluded a workshop with colleagues, capturing key process steps in a whiteboarding tool, a PDF document, or a Word file. Instead of manually converting this information into a BPMN model, you can now input the text directly into SAP Signavio, and AI will generate an initial process model for you. If needed, you can regenerate the model from scratch or fine-tune the generated diagram. 

What makes a good prompt? 

To ensure the best possible BPMN diagram generation, a good prompt should follow these best practices:  

  • Define the process scope 
    • Clearly state the objective of the process. 
    • Identify start and end points, along with any key milestones.  
  • Structure the process logically  
    • Describe a Start Event to indicate where the process begins.  
    • Break the process into clear activities or tasks.  
    • Use a sequential order that mirrors real-world execution.  
  • Include decision points and conditions  
    • Specify conditions that determine different pathways.  
    • Define actions based on different outcomes (e.g., approval vs. rejection scenarios).  
  • Ensure accuracy and completeness  
    • Provide sufficient detail while avoiding unnecessary complexity.  
    • Make sure key actors, dependencies, and interactions are clearly stated 

Example of a good prompt: 

"The process starts when a payroll data alert is assigned. The agent assesses the alert. If no data change is needed, the agent comments and closes the alert. If a data change is required, the agent consults with the line manager, determines if an update is needed, and corrects the payroll data if necessary. If additional updates are required, the agent works with the system administrator to finalize changes before closing the alert." 

BPMN diagram output 

 

 Benefits 

Process modeling for an enterprise can cost hundreds of thousands of dollars annually, factoring in the time it takes to collect inputs, create or update hundreds of process models. By leveraging the AI-assisted process modeler, text to process capability, this amount can be reduced significantly while giving time back to then be used to customers advantage. 

Key benefits include: 

  • Faster time to value: Rapidly generate process models from text, reducing the time spent on manual diagram creation. 
  • Enhanced collaboration: Simplify complex process descriptions by turning them into visual models, improving cross-team communication. 
  • Flexible editing: Ensure continuous improvement of models with the ability to save and refine them in SAP Signavio Process Manager. 

Availability and activation 

The AI-assisted process modeler, text to process feature is planned to be generally available in March 2025 (subject to change). Reach out to your account executive or customer success partner for updates on release date and more information on how to access this capability. Also visit the roadmap explorer for further details.    

The future of AI in process modeling 

This is just the beginning of an exciting journey towards AI-driven process modeling. In the coming months, SAP Signavio will continue to introduce innovative AI-powered features, enhancing productivity and enabling more intuitive process management. 

Stay tuned for more updates and start transforming the way you model business processes with AI!

entry.title: 11 great Business AI prototypes at the Capgemini private Hack2Build!
entry.content:
Introduction

The Hack2Build initiative is traditionally a 7-day challenge for multiple partners to build working prototypes. At first each partner's submitted use case is evaluated for technical feasibility and customer relevance by SAP. Then SAP experts provide guidance and coaching to partners with accepted use cases. Finally partners are invited to demo their prototype to a panel of judges in a demo jam. 

Our partner Capgemini is obviously taking Business AI very seriously and approached us shortly after SAP Sapphire 2024 with 40+ Business AI use cases across SAP business portfolio, prepared by various teams around the world. A first review showed the potential so we jointly decided to kick off the Capgemini private Hack2Build: an initiative to help Capgemini develop Business AI solutions that are relevant, reliable, and responsible for the 10-15 most promising use cases.

About the Capgemini private Hack2Build Initiative

Capgemini used SAP AI Core along with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) services, including SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine's Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). These capabilities helped contextualize information with any of the available 23 Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide enterprise-grade responses. Capgemini teams also utilised existing business services to enhance their AI solutions.

The initiative ran from November 19-27, 2024 in an hybrid mode with a few teams working out of the SAPLabs France in Mougins with a few SAP experts together while other teams connected remotely from around the world with the entire SAP team.

The event concluded with a demo jam where Capgemini teams showcased their working prototypes that integrated SAP BTP and SAP AI Core services with both SAP and non-SAP data. The demo jam session produced a winner and two runners-up. This blog highlights the use cases developed by the 3 winning teams as well as the other 8 finalists.

 

Kitchen captAIn
GDPR CompliBuddy
Material Master Data Maintenance
Intelligent Capacity Scheduling
Accelerated GxP Compliance Documentation
Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Modelling,Planning
Emergency Response Simulator
SAP CX - Hyper personalization
GenAI-powered vendor invoice management
Augmented field worker
Intelligent Contract Management

Winning Solutions

The top 3 solutions selected by the panel of judges were:

🥇  Augmented Field Worker
🥈  Accelerated GxP Compliance
🥉  Intelligent Material Master Data Maintenance

 

🥇  Augmented Field Worker

The solution leverages Generative AI, SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), and HANA Database with Vector Engine to automate Non-Conformity (NC) qualification and resolution. It empowers field engineers with a mobile app providing instant similarity analysis based on a picture from a suspected NC, voice-activated commands e.g. with access to necessary documentation to declare an NC, and real-time repair recommendations, streamlining workflows, reducing manual effort, and enabling faster, more accurate decision-making in challenging field environments.

 

🥈  Accelerated GxP Compliance

Accelerated Good Practices (GxP) Compliance Tool provides GenAI-powered pharma documentation capabilities by connecting with core SAP processes via APIs to extract key data points. GenAI drafts initial content based on pre-defined templates and automates data entry based on pharma guidelines and company baselines, ensuring compliance to regulatory bodies and GxP norms.

 

🥉  Intelligent Material Master Data Maintenance

Intelligent Material Master Data Maintenance is a RAG-based SAP solution that helps to reduce of manual workload and errors, supports faster onboarding of materials using AI, increases data accuracy and reduces risks and costs in the supply chain.

 

Finalists

Below a short description of the 8 finalist solutions in no particular order

Kitchen captAIn

Kitchen captAIn is an intelligent solution catering to grocery-based platforms. It provides grocery/product details to customers with specific recipe/menu ideas, highlights details like allergy ans calorie details based on preferences to ensure an optimal and personalized buying experience while maximizing grocery utilization and minimizing waste.

GenAI Hyper personalization

 GenAI Hyperonalization collects real-time clickstream data from website visitors, including country, locations, cart items, purchases, duration, etc. It utilizes the captured data to create dynamic user personas for each session. Business users can generate personalized content and images for the products and delivering instant, tailored content directly on the store front.

Intelligent Contract Management

Intelligent Contract Management provides GenAI-powered contract management capabilities. It connects SAP Ariba processes with APIs to extract key data point, create and review draft contracts with intelligent insights from Ariba and S4HANA, and augment Ariba's Clauses library with new clauses.

Smart Field Report

Smart Field Report addresses the challenges of manual, time-consuming reports generation in Oil & Gas upstream operations e.g. production, drilling, emissions, by automating data extraction, analysis, and reporting on SAP and non-SAP relevant data. It improves efficiency by eliminating human errors, provides accurate and consistent reports, and offers actionable insights for better decision-/recommendations-making. The solution also enables real-time operations, ensures compliance, and drives digital transformation, promoting smarter and data-driven operations.

 

Intelligent Capacity Scheduling

Intelligent Capacity Scheduling tackles production scheduling challenges by using Generative AI to create a dynamic and intelligent scheduling system. It enhances SAP current setup by making schedules more adaptive and data-driven.

 

Panel of Judges

We extend our sincere thanks to the judges who lent their expertise to this initiative. Senior leaders and experts from SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and Partner Ecosystem Success carefully evaluated 6-minute pitches from participating teams. They assessed each solution’s potential to address real-world challenges using SAP Business AI and provided thoughtful feedback to help partners further refine their solutions in the next phase of Hack2Build.

Helen Kushakovska

SAPLabs Mougins Managing Director

Christian Koenig

Director - Business AI Partner Product Management

Gianluigi Bagnoli

Global VP, BTP Solution Architects, PES

Rudi Held

Vice-President, Global Head of SAP Co-Innovation Lab, PES

Special Thanks

We extend our appreciation to the SAP team members from SAP Business AI, BTP Architecture, Strategic Partner Enablement, and SAP Co-Innovation Lab, whose expertise guided partners to successfully develop their solutions during the Capgemini Private Hack2Build Business AI initiative. Special thanks as well to the entire SAPLabs France team in Mougins for hosting us in their nice facilities.

Way Forward

Hack2Build consists of two phases: the hack phase and the build phase. All finalist teams in this initiative continue working with SAP, maintaining momentum in this co-innovation journey. SAP engages with Capgemini to expand the business AI prototypes into comprehensive offerings for customers.

As of today 3 solutions already made it (below) and 6 are in the process:

Augmented field workerValidated Partner Use Case (VPUC)
Smart Field ReportValidated Partner Use Case (VPUC)
Kitchen captAIn

Validated Partner Use Case (VPUC)

Video

Please click on the VIDEO LINK  for a first impression how the initiative looked like.

 

SAP AI Core SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Deliver AI value across your business with DataRobot Insight Apps for SAP
entry.content:
At DataRobot, we’ve worked with hundreds of clients across industries and one thing remains crystal clear: delivering AI to where users need it, and where they’ll actually use it, is still a challenge. As organizations work to fit AI into their business processes, they’re still running into the same big hurdles. 

Organizations struggle with unifying data and getting it AI-ready, and they struggle with building AI solutions that solve pressing business problems, and delivering those solutions to users in time to make a real impact. Instead of focusing on what needs to be delivered, they get bogged down with making data accessible, creating the infrastructure for AI deployment, and designing solutions.

With the launch of SAP Business Data Cloud, and the AI-powered Insight Apps for SAP from DataRobot, it’s never been easier to move past infrastructure constraints, and focus on delivering real business value from AI. 

This joint solution provides the fastest way to unify data access, operationalize AI, and deliver AI where it’s needed – transforming how organizations work with AI.

Enhancing SAP Business Data Cloud with Insight Apps 

SAP Business Data Cloud unifies data and analytics with companies’ business processes, empowering leaders with complete context for faster decision making and better business planning. This serves as a trusted data foundation, delivering seamless access to reliable, business-ready data and empowering organizations to prepare data for AI and drive innovation with confidence. 

To help organizations leverage this unified data and rapidly deliver relevant and impactful AI solutions, DataRobot is launching AI-powered Insight Apps for Business Data Cloud. 

These new application suites combine deep industry domain experience and best practices with pre-built and customizable solutions that accelerate use cases for planning, enterprise analytics, advanced analytical apps, and predictive forecasting. 

This enables SAP customers to prioritize business needs and deliver solutions built with trusted methods, and end-to-end observability and oversight. 

DataRobot Insight Apps provide the fastest way to confidently develop and deliver critical AI use cases directly where they’re needed.

Each DataRobot Insight App comes complete with the key components to tackle critical use cases:

  • Multiple specialized, pre-built and customizable solutions tailored to specific industry vertical or horizontal use cases such as cash flow forecasting or inventory management
  • One-click integrations with data sources like Business Data Cloud and others, reducing the effort needed to connect systems
  • Proprietary AI logic built on a decade of AI experience, designed for quick deployment and accuracy at scale
  • A user-centric design that can be deployed as fully customized applications or integrated into existing systems like SAP Analytics Cloud or Integrated Business Planning.

Empowering finance with AI-powered insights 

Finance teams face enormous pressure to make accurate, data-driven decisions in an environment of increasing complexity and uncertainty. They must analyze vast amounts of data to forecast outcomes, guide strategic planning, and optimize financial operations. 

To be truly effective, these insights need to be accurate at scale and delivered in time for the business to act.

The DataRobot Insight App for Finance bridges the gap between raw data and actionable insights through seamless integration with SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, unlocking and optimizing critical use cases across the office of finance. 

This means finance teams can integrate AI-driven forecasts directly into their finance operations, enabling teams to swiftly transform disparate and complex data into reliable insights, leading to faster and more confident decision-making. 

By augmenting critical tasks such as revenue forecasting, budget analysis, and cash flow management, teams can accelerate the delivery of impactful AI while reducing operational costs and risk. 

With ready-to-use solutions for use cases like cash flow forecasting, treasury teams can rapidly deploy solutions that enable end users to access higher quality, invoice-level forecasts precisely where they need them, providing deeper insights into cash inflows and outflows for both the short term and long term. 

This ensures finance leaders can start making more accurate operational cash management decisions in a matter of weeks. Once they have one solution in production, they can quickly scale across the department and the organization. 

Optimizing supply chains with AI-driven forecasting 

Supply chain teams face similar pressure to their FP&A counterparts, as they work to use forecasting to minimize disruption, navigate uncertainty, and ensure operational efficiency. Achieving accuracy at scale and across geographies becomes increasingly difficult as the number of products and stores grows, often requiring dozens or even hundreds of forecasts. 

Even more difficult is ensuring predictions arrive in time to take action and remain consistent with other forecasts. 

The Insight App for supply chain from DataRobot addresses core challenges across the supply chain by combining automated model development with streamlined configuration and process governance. With built-in one-click deployment, these solutions can be seamlessly infused into existing planning workflows in IBP or surfaced in SAC dashboards. This accelerates the first step for our customers to incorporate the latest AI technology, and build a more sustainable and resilient supply chain.

How a DataRobot transformed a global brewery’s supply chain

When the COVID-19 pandemic struck, one of the world’s largest breweries encountered a significant challenge. Their hand-coded models for predicting demand across 20+ markets became ineffective, leading to an imbalance in supply. While beer was flying off retail shelves, it sat untouched in bars and restaurants, costing the company $110 million annually.

The traditional hand-coded approach not only lacked accuracy but also required six months to implement for each use case, making it impossible to scale solutions in time to address the crisis. To overcome this, the brewery turned to DataRobot’s AI capabilities to better connect business data and streamline processes.

By leveraging DataRobot, the brewery reduced model deployment time from six months to just two, rapidly scaling the solution across six global markets. In just five weeks, these advanced models generated over $17 million in additional beer sales, transforming their global supply chain and ensuring they could meet demand with precision.

Put business-ready AI into the hands of your users 

The next era of AI will be defined by delivering AI where users work and how they work. Whether you’re a current SAP customer or just beginning your AI journey, the future of AI value lies in solving business problems and delivering those solutions exactly where they’re needed.

SAP and DataRobot have been focused on removing barriers to this process by helping you simplify access to your data and streamline the delivering of AI apps into the SAP products your teams use every day.

Interested to learn more? Check out DataRobot’s launch webinar featuring SAP.

We also recently published our survey that provides an in-depth view of the hurdles that are preventing AI teams from delivering value.

entry.title: Importance of “Right” Education in the AI Era
entry.content:
Co-Authored by: Aruna K (Aruna.k@sap.com) 

 

 

Premise

Not since the days of Italian Bologna University (one of the earliest universities in the world which was founded in 1088) or the Indian Nalanda University (427 CE) are we ever so “unclear” and “unsure” of the academic relevance of traditional teaching and learning.

With the invent of AI and its tools, every book-based content is not only consumable but can be represented in the format that best fits an academic course without having to ever “read to understand” it.

The traditional teaching systems followed by tests and exams that focus on the ability of students to “remember than understand” will soon be rendered meaningless.

If the current generation of kids are not exposed to the right way of learning, the academics coming out of colleges will be directionless as to what is to make of their degrees and certificates.

 

Learn to Teach – Teach to Learn

The inevitability of AI can be felt from how we operate our day-to-day electronic devices to how our doctors treat our illness.

There is no point running away from this innovation and nor can we dismiss the fact that, certain jobs which involves clear repeatable activities would soon be achieved through machines which can perform these tasks tirelessly and without any errors.

What is to be understood is, AI after all is still a machine and may not reach a stage of human evolution of reasoning and empathy (Let’s hope it doesn’t and if it indeed does, it does in the right way for the sake of humanity).

The right attitude for everyone including educational institutions is to learn to live with AI and leverage AI to better us and the society.

In an era where artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly transforming industries and daily life, the education system must evolve to equip students with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in this new landscape. This document outlines key areas where academic curricula should adapt to ensure that students are prepared to work effectively alongside AI, develop unique skills, and contribute to ethical innovations in technology. By addressing these points, we can create a more relevant and forward-thinking education system.

1. Learn to Work Effectively Alongside AI

As AI becomes an integral part of various fields, students and more importantly teachers and professors should be trained to collaborate with AI tools and systems. This includes understanding how to leverage AI for data analysis, decision-making, and creative processes, ensuring that everyone can enhance their productivity and innovation through technology.

2. Develop Skills That AI Cannot Easily Replicate

While AI excels at processing data and performing repetitive tasks, there are certain human skills that remain irreplaceable. Education should focus on fostering creativity, emotional intelligence, critical thinking, and interpersonal skills—areas where humans have a distinct advantage over machines.

3. Be Ready to Quickly Adapt to a Rapidly Evolving AI Ecosystem

The pace of AI development is unprecedented, and students as well as teachers must be prepared to adapt to new tools and technologies as they emerge. Curricula should emphasize lifelong learning and adaptability, encouraging students to stay informed about advancements in AI and related fields.

4. Contribute to Ethical AI Innovations

As AI continues to influence society, it is crucial for students to understand the ethical implications of their work. Education should include discussions on responsible AI development, bias in algorithms, and the societal impact of technology, empowering students to contribute to ethical innovations.

5. Be Open to Adjusting and Correcting Syllabus More Frequently

The current education system often relies on outdated syllabi that remain unchanged for years. To keep pace with technological advancements, educational institutions should adopt a more flexible approach, regularly updating course content to reflect the latest developments in AI and its applications. Central government should intervene by introducing robust curriculum early into the education system.

6. Create a System for Discussing Morality and Ethics of AI

Students should engage in discussions about the moral and ethical dimensions of AI. This includes exploring questions about privacy, security, and the potential consequences of AI decisions. Creating a forum for these discussions will help students develop a nuanced understanding of the responsibilities that come with technological advancement.

7. Focus on Sustainability and Technology's Role in Environmental Challenges

Education should also address the intersection of technology and sustainability. Students should learn how AI can be harnessed to tackle environmental challenges, such as climate change and resource management, fostering a sense of responsibility towards the planet.

8. Choose Areas Where AI Application Has More Scope

Finally, educational institutions should guide students towards fields where AI applications are expanding. This includes areas like healthcare, finance, and logistics, where AI can significantly enhance efficiency and effectiveness. By aligning academic programs with industry needs, students can better prepare for future job markets.

In conclusion, adapting the education system to incorporate these pointers will not only prepare students for a future dominated by AI but also empower them to become responsible innovators and leaders in their fields. By fostering a curriculum that emphasizes collaboration with AI, ethical considerations, and sustainability, we can ensure that the next generation is equipped to navigate the complexities of an AI-driven world.

Conclusion

The “fear” of technology replacing humans and their jobs is not something new. Be it machines to enable supply chain, or vehicles to carry people around or a simple machine that washes your clothes, all these innovations bought with it the concern that human jobs would be at stake.

But we humans have been resilient enough to “incorporate” these machines into our lives and find newer better work to do.

The hope of this blog is to still believe in humanities’ ever evolving resilience to improve the type of work we do and incorporate Artificial Intelligence into to our day-to-day activities to lead a better life.

The current blog focuses on necessary steps to be taken in the early parts of education system (age group: 5 to 15). Students in higher education and colleges are anyway expected to catch up with latest trends. The idea of this blog is to ensure education on AI starts early for the kids to be better prepared for the future that’s coming up!

entry.title: Monitoring & Tracing LLM Calls at Scale: Achieving Observability with SAP Cloud Logging Service
entry.content:
1. Introduction

Generative AI (GenAI) projects often rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) to power chatbots, content generation, and text analysis applications. Despite the immense potential of these solutions, they can be challenging to monitor and troubleshoot due to high volumes of calls, token-based billing, and complex microservice interactions.

SAP Cloud Logging service is an instance-based observability solution built on OpenSearch, designed to store, visualize, and analyze application logs, metrics, and traces from SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, and other runtime environments. By collecting telemetry data from your LLM-based applications—whether via OpenTelemetry (OTel) or a JSON ingestion endpoint—you gain comprehensive insights into load, latency, costs, and error trends. This blog outlines two approaches to integrate your GenAI solutions with SAP Cloud Logging Service so you can maintain robust observability.

Note: SAP Cloud Logging Service supports additional methods of integration as well; for more details, refer to the official SAP Help documentation for SAP Cloud Logging.

2. Why Observability Matters for GenAI LLMs

  1. Cost and Token Billing
    Many commercial LLMs charge per token (input + output). Monitoring token usage helps you budget effectively and optimize prompts to reduce costs.

  2. Real-time Troubleshooting
    LLM applications may fail due to timeouts, malformed inputs, or intermittent API issues. Centralized logs and traces let you identify where and why these failures occur.

  3. Latency and Performance
    LLM latency affects user experience. Monitoring start-to-finish inference times reveals bottlenecks, whether in the LLM itself or upstream/downstream services.

  4. Compliance and Accountability
    Observability can help ensure the generated text adheres to organizational or regulatory requirements, providing traceability for auditing and post-mortem analysis.

3. Technology Stack Overview

  • SAP Cloud Logging Service
    A managed platform based on OpenSearch that stores logs, metrics, and traces from various runtimes (e.g., Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes). It provides predefined dashboards for investigating load, latency, and error patterns, plus the flexibility to build custom views.

  • OpenTelemetry (OTel)
    An open-source, vendor-neutral suite of APIs, libraries, and tooling to capture telemetry data from distributed systems. It standardizes how logs, metrics, and traces are generated, processed, and exported.

Both ingestion options detailed below work well with SAP Cloud Logging Service, and the choice depends on your technical requirements, existing infrastructure, and developer preferences.

4. Monitoring LLM Calls: Two Ingestion Approaches

Prerequisites

Before integrating LLM call monitoring with SAP Cloud Logging Service, ensure the following:

  • Create a Cloud Logging Instance: You need an SAP Cloud Logging instance before ingestion can begin. Follow the initial setup guide to configure it correctly.
  • Enable OpenTelemetry (OTel) if Using the OTel API:
    • Cloud Logging instances must be OTel-enabled to receive traces and logs via OTel.
    • Enable OTel ingestion via configuring the following parameter: 
      {
    "ingest_otlp": {
        "enabled": "true"
    }
}
      Follow the steps outlined in the OpenTelemetry ingestion documentation for more details.
  • Create a Service Key: A service key is required for authentication. Learn how to create an SAP Cloud Logging instance and generate a Service Key.

This section presents two approaches to integrate your LLM calls with SAP Cloud Logging Service. Each method captures key details (like token usage and latency).

4.1 Ingestion via OpenTelemetry (OTel)

4.1.1 Overview

Using OTel lets you instrument, generate, and export logs/traces in a structured manner. Many organizations already adopt OTel for unified observability, making it straightforward to add LLM telemetry to existing pipelines.

4.1.2 Setup Steps

  1. Obtain Your Service Key

    • You need to create and then download a Cloud Logging service key, which is a JSON file containing credentials for the SAP Cloud Logging Service.
    • This file includes fields like ingest-otlp-cert, ingest-otlp-key, and ingest-otlp-endpoint.

  2. Initialize OTel in Your Code
    Below is an example in Python:

    import grpc
import json
from opentelemetry import trace
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor
from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.grpc.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter

def init_otel():
    """Initialize OTel with SAP Cloud Logging Service credentials."""
    resource = Resource(attributes={
        "service.name": "genai-llm-service",
        "environment": "production"
    })
    tracer_provider = TracerProvider(resource=resource)

    with open('secrets/cloud-logging-sk.json', 'r') as f:
        service_key = json.load(f)

    ssl_credentials = grpc.ssl_channel_credentials(
        private_key=service_key["ingest-otlp-key"].encode(),
        certificate_chain=service_key["ingest-otlp-cert"].encode()
    )
    exporter = OTLPSpanExporter(
        endpoint=service_key["ingest-otlp-endpoint"],
        credentials=ssl_credentials
    )

    span_processor = BatchSpanProcessor(exporter)
    tracer_provider.add_span_processor(span_processor)

    # Set global tracer provider
    trace.set_tracer_provider(tracer_provider)

  3. Instrument Your LLM Calls
    Wrap each call in a span to record start/end times, model name, and token usage:

    import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from opentelemetry.trace import get_tracer

tracer = get_tracer(__name__)

def call_llm(oauth_token, prompt, model_name="gpt-35-turbo"):
    start_time = time.time()
    iso_start = datetime.fromtimestamp(start_time, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()

    with tracer.start_as_current_span("llm_inference_span") as span:
        span.set_attribute("start_time", iso_start)
        span.set_attribute("model_name", model_name)

        payload = {"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt}
        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {oauth_token}"}
        response = requests.post("https://api.example.com/llm-endpoint", json=payload, headers=headers)

        end_time = time.time()
        iso_end = datetime.fromtimestamp(end_time, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()
        latency = end_time - start_time

        span.set_attribute("end_time", iso_end)
        span.set_attribute("response_latency", latency)
        span.set_attribute("status_code", str(response.status_code))
        span.set_attribute("request_body", payload)

        # Parse response
        try:
            json_resp = response.json()
        except ValueError:
            json_resp = {"error": "Invalid JSON response"}
        span.set_attribute("response_body", json_resp)

        # Example token usage
        span.set_attribute("input_text_token_count", 100)
        span.set_attribute("output_text_token_count", 20)
        span.set_attribute("total_text_token_count", 120)

    return response.text

  4. Verify in SAP Cloud Logging Service

    Once your application is sending telemetry data, follow these steps to view it in SAP Cloud Logging Service:

    1. Run your application to start sending telemetry data.
    2. Access the dashboard endpoint (included in the service key).
    3. View your data in SAP Cloud Logging Service:
      • Open the Discover tab in SAP Cloud Logging Service.
      • Select your OpenTelemetry indices, e.g., otel-v1-apm-span-* for traces/spans.
      • Navigate to the pre-built OpenTelemetry dashboards, which provide visualizations for server spans, logs, and metrics.
      • Alternatively, create custom dashboards to track request throughput, latency, and error codes.

    For detailed instructions, refer to the OTel ingestion documentation.

4.2 Ingestion via JSON Endpoint

4.2.1 Overview

If you prefer to manage logs as structured JSON documents or have a scenario where OTel libraries are not fully applicable, you can directly send JSON payloads to the logging service. Both methods ultimately store data in SAP Cloud Logging Service; the choice depends on technical constraints or personal preference.

4.2.2 Setup Steps

  1. Obtain Your Service Key

    • You need to create and then download a Cloud Logging service key, which is a JSON file with fields like ingest-mtls-cert, ingest-mtls-key, server-ca, and ingest-mtls-endpoint.
    • This is used for mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication.

  2. Prepare mTLS Certificates

    • Write ingest-mtls-cert, ingest-mtls-key, and server-ca to local files (e.g., client.crt, client.key, server-ca.crt).
    • Secure these files according to best practices.

  3. Construct JSON Payload

    • Include fields relevant to LLM observability (e.g., serviceName, timestamps, model name, and token counts).
    • Example:
    import json
import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def log_llm_call_json(prompt, model_name):
    start_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "serviceName": "genai-llm-service",
        "startTime": start_time,
        "span.attributes.start_time": start_time,
        "span.attributes.model_name": model_name,
        "span.attributes.request_body": {"prompt": prompt},
        "span.attributes.input_text_token_count": 100,
        "span.attributes.output_text_token_count": 20,
        "span.attributes.total_text_token_count": 120
        # Add other fields like end_time, response_latency, etc. as needed
    }

    with open('secrets/cloud-logging-sk.json', 'r') as f:
        creds = json.load(f)

    # Build URL with the correct suffix /customindex/llm-calls-logs
    url = f"https://{creds['ingest-mtls-endpoint']}/customindex/llm-calls-logs"
    cert_files = ("secrets/client.crt", "secrets/client.key")

    response = requests.put(
        url,
        data=json.dumps(payload),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        cert=cert_files,
        verify="secrets/server-ca.crt"
    )
    response.raise_for_status()
    print("Log entry sent successfully.")

  4. Verify in SAP Cloud Logging Service

    Once your application is sending logs via PUT, access the dashboard endpoint (provided in the service key file) and follow these steps to verify the data in SAP Cloud Logging Service:

    1. Open the Discover tab in SAP Cloud Logging Service.
    2. Select the logs-json-* index to locate your ingested data.
    3. Alternatively, create queries or dashboards to track usage, latency, and error rates.

    For more details, refer to the JSON ingestion documentation.

5. Best Practices

Regardless of your chosen ingestion approach, keep in mind:

  1. Initialize Only Once
    If using OTel, avoid multiple calls to your initialization function. Repeated setups can cause duplicate logging or performance overhead.

  2. Secure Your Credentials
    Whether using OTel or JSON ingestion, treat your service key, certificates, and private keys as sensitive.

6. Conclusion

Observability is the backbone of robust, efficient GenAI LLM solutions—helping you diagnose issues, and maintain strong service performance. With SAP Cloud Logging Service, you have a powerful, scalable environment to store and analyze logs, metrics, and traces from any environment or runtime. Meanwhile, both the OpenTelemetry approach and the JSON Endpoint approach can reliably feed your GenAI telemetry data into the service.

By choosing the ingestion method that fits your needs, and implementing best practices around security and dashboards, you’ll ensure your LLM-powered applications deliver timely, accurate, and compliant responses at scale.

Further Reading

  • SAP Help (SAP Cloud Logging)
    https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-logging/cloud-logging/what-is-sap-cloud-logging
  • OpenTelemetry Documentation
    https://opentelemetry.io/

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 3: SAP SuccessFactors (AI, Joule and More)
entry.content:
This third post in my series gives an overview of user experience innovations in SAP SuccessFactors. It covers Joule and many AI-based UX innovations along with enhanced user experiences. By following the career of Emma, who starts by applying for a job and then grows within the company, you can see how Generative AI embedded in business applications helps business users work more effectively, giving them unprecedented insights and convenience.

Before reading this, do read the first blog post introducing the series and providing general information about Joule and central entry points and services, the AI patterns presented here, along with links to all the published parts of this blog post series:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

SAP SuccessFactors

The 2H2024 release comes with a large number of UX innovations across the whole portfolio, including more than 30 new AI capabilities and over 15 new Joule capabilities.

In the following sections, I’ll summarize the main highlights by following the career of Emma, and showing how the new AI-powered UX innovations help:

  • Emma to find the right job which match her skills when applying to join the company.
  • Her manager and HR colleagues to set up onboarding for her, i.e. writing welcome message, supported by the AI writing assistant, and defining required activities.
  • Emma and her manager to manage day-to-day requests and approvals directly in Microsoft Teams, such as requesting or approving time off, approving time sheets. Both benefit from natural language search in Microsoft Teams.
  • Emma to learn new skills with the new learning experience.
  • Managers to formulate new assignments to extend their teams.
  • Emma to advance further in her career by recommending potential new roles which build on her current skills, as well as giving her career insights about which skill she needs to grow to get to her chosen new role.
  • Her manager to give her well-formulated, unbiased and actionable feedback, supported by the AI writing assistant. This helps Emma grow in her job.
  • Her manager to get an overview of Emma’s compensation, with an AI-generated summary, including information about where she stands compared to others.

 To get the complete picture of all the new SuccessFactors innovations, have a look at these two overviews, plus this must-see video summarizing the AI innovations:

  • SAP SuccessFactors HCM 2H 2024 Release Highlights.
  • Second Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile.
  • Video: Business AI in SAP SuccessFactors – 2H 2024 Release Highlights (3:43 min.).

 In case you haven’t already seen the overview of UX innovations introduced with the 1H2024 release, have a look at my blog post from last July:

  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.

AI Helping Candidates Find the Right Job Based on their Skills

Emma is looking for a new job in a different company and would like to quickly identify those jobs which best match her skills. In the past this was a painstaking task, scanning all the promising-sounding jobs and trying to work out which fitted best. But Emma is applying to a company using SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting, and wow: all she has to do is upload her resume, and AI extracts the skills and shows the open roles which best match her skills. Even better, the AI-assisted job skills feature provides a view of the specific skills required for each role, giving candidates insight into additional opportunities. These skills, generated directly from job descriptions, are displayed on the job details page for easy reference, as you can see in Figure 1. This an example of the Guiding and Recommending AI pattern.

Figure 1: AI-assisted skills-matching for candidates applying for jobs using SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting. ALT Text: The image shows two screenshots. On the left a Skills Matching screen with a popover for uploading a resume. Below the field with the filename of the uploaded resume, a green success message reads “Resume checked”. Below that 40 skills are listed in six rows, one button per skill. The popover has two action buttons: “View Jobs” and “Cancel”. The screen on the right shows the list of recommended jobs, with filters “My Skills”, “USA” and “California” selected: five jobs are listed, all with an indicator “Skills Matched”.

AI-Assisted Onboarding of New Hires

Let us now assume that Emma has been hired for the new job. (A reminder here: her manager would have been able to use AI-assistance in generating the interview questions, as I highlighted in my blog post from July 2024.)

Her manager can now use the AI copilot Joule to accelerate task completion and effectively manage tasks. Joule allows hiring managers and HR colleagues to view and initiate pending tasks, such as data review, forms, and e-signatures. It also offers a conversational way for new hires to complete activities and receive recommendations and guidance during onboarding. Figure 2 shows an example.

Hiring managers and HR teams can leverage AI-assisted writing for welcome and farewell messages, ensuring good communication tone and clarity, and avoiding unconscious bias (I’ll tell you more about the writing assistant in the section on performance feedback below), as you can see in Figure X. (An example of the Creating Proposals AI pattern).

A New Hire Onboarding guided tour, enabled by SAP Companion, is now available from the Open Help icon in the Onboarding Checklist. The guided tour assists first time or unfamiliar users with navigating through their onboarding tasks, helping improve completion rates and reduce time to hire. You can see an example in Figure 2, or watch it in action here:

  • Video: Enhancements to New Hire Onboarding Journey (1:03 min.)

Figure 2: AI-assisted onboarding in SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Three screenshots are shown: First from Joule, where the user asked “Show my pending tasks for onboarding”, and received three tasks (New Hire Data Review, Complete Section 2 of Form 1-9, Final Review), each with a “Select” button. The user selected “New Hire Data Review” and received a list of three new hires, with a “View More” link. Second a “Write Welcome Message” screen where an initial text is visible along with a drop-down list with entries “Enhance Writing”, “Change Tone” (with sub-entries “Make More Professional; Personable; Constructive”, “Make Shorter”, “Make Longer”, “Make Bulleted List” and “Translate”. Third, “Your Onboarding Checklist” showing a list of tasks, with “Onboarding Data Collection” selected and showing a form on the right for entering personal data.

Find out more here:

  • 2H 2024: AI-Driven Innovations to Streamline Productivity in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

SAP SuccessFactors Requests and Approvals in Microsoft Teams

Emma uses Microsoft Teams for collaborating with colleagues at work, so spends quite a lot of time in that environment. With the SAP SuccessFactors 2H2024 release, she can now, directly in Microsoft Teams:

  • Submit time-off requests;
  • View upcoming absences;
  • Sync holidays maintained in SAP SuccessFactors to her calendar in Microsoft Teams.

Similarly, her manager can

  • approve time-off requests directly in Microsoft Teams;
  • delegate performance appraisals to colleagues to help manage responsibilities on each other’s behalf.

All employees can use the improved natural language natural-language search function now available in Microsoft Teams, which makes it easy to find tasks and information and initiate action with simple language commands.

Figure 3: Approve SAP SuccessFactors time sheet and change of position in Microsoft Teams. ALT Text: A screenshot of Microsoft Teams with a card listing 12 actions in four rows: Manage My Data, Manage My Team, Request Feedback, Give Feedback, Clock My Time, View My Pay Statement, Create Activity, Delegate My Workflow, Request Time Off, Sync Absences to Calendar, Sync Holidays to Calendar, Recognize. Below that a card showing an approved time sheet for a week. At the bottom a card “Change Position” with the status in red “Approval Pending”. The card contains information about the position, dates effective on and submitted on as well as submitted by, and provides “Approve” and “Approve with Comments” buttons.

AI-Assisted Learning

In this release AI tools help learning administrators maintain their organization’s skills-based learning attributes.

For Emma, this means that she gets AI-based recommendations for learnings that suit her individual profile, as well as mandatory trainings. We have enhanced the SAP SuccessFactors mobile app so that Emma can download learning content, as well as receiving these recommendations and information about new and required learnings – as shown in Figure 4. This is also an example of the Guiding and Recommending AI pattern.

Figure 4: Enhanced learning experience with the SAP SuccessFactors mobile app. ALT Text: The image shows three mobile phone examples of Learning – New Learning Experience. On the left “My Downloads” showing a card with “Employee health and wellness …” due on 11/18/24, with a “Launch Content” button. Below that an illustrated image with text “Your device is offline. You can still make progress on any downloaded content…” In the middle a screen showing a new offering, with a “Learn More” button, and a section “Required learning” showing one card and a “View All (7)” link. On the right a section “Latest Bookmarks” with a “Topic of the Month” card, and a section “Suggested for Me” and a “View All (12)” link, showing a card “Standards of Ethical Conduct”.

Emma’s manager benefits from the initial release of a new refreshed manager view which helps to quickly identify and address action items, such as outstanding learning activities that need to be completed.

This blog post gives an overview of additional new enhancements in usability of SAP SuccessFactors Learning:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Learning: What’s New in the 2H 2024 Release.

AI-Assisted Assignment Creation

Managers looking to attract employees to internal assignments can now use AI-assisted assignment creation to make their lives easier: just write a descriptive sentence or two describing the assignment you want to create, and generate a proposal assignment in SAP SuccessFactors (Figure 5). This is another example of the Creating Proposals AI pattern.

Figure 5: AI-assisted assignment creation in SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: a screenshot “Create Assignment” with “Hi, Daniela Olivera” in the middle, above a label “Tell us what you want to create:” above a text entry field containing “We are initiating a six-months long machine learning project and are in search of 2 highly skilled software engineer.”, next to a “Generate” button containing the AI icon. Below that a label “Other ways to create an assignment”, above two wide selection panels “Create from Scratch” and “Copy from Existing Assignments”.

Should a manager want to fill a new position, rather than a short-term assignment, they can use the AI-assisted generation of job descriptions which was introduced a year ago (see my February 2024 blog post )

AI-Assisted Role Recommendations and Career Insights

After a few years at the company, Emma is confident that she can grow into a more demanding role. But which ones make sense for her? SAP SuccessFactors now provides AI assistance by making recommendations for roles which she could well grow into based on her current skills. Figure 6 shows what this looks like, with the Top Matches shown in a card carousel.

Figure 6: AI-Assisted Role Recommendations with SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development. ALT Text: Screenshot of “Explore Career Options” showing a row of selectable cards with illustrations for “Top Matches”, another row for “Next Career Move” and the top half of a third row “Trending Roles”. The user’s mouse pointer is over the “Sustainable Innovation Lead” card.

She decides to look at the role Sustainable Innovation Lead. When selecting it, she is taken to an overview of the role. There she sees which skills and attributes she already has for the role, along with those which she needs to work on to improve in order to take on the role (Figure 7).

Figure 7: The role overview with SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development. ALT Text: Screenshot of Growth Portfolio displaying the role “Sustainable Innovation Lead”. The top of the screen has a section “About This Role” with one line of text. Below that a section “Skills and Attributes to Improve” showing a row of four cards showing a horizontal line chart for proficiency on a scale of 0 to 5, with an indicator showing the necessary skill level for the role, and the chart showing the user’s current skill level. Below that a section “Skills and Attributes You Have” showing a similar row of four cards. The user’s mouse pointer is over the button “Add as Target Role”.

After adding this a target role, she would like to find out more about how she can best go about improving the skills which have been mentioned as needing improvement. Here SAP SuccessFactors is again at hand with AI-assistance. As you can see in Figure 8, after the previous step of adding the role as a target role she is given the option to generate AI-powered career insights. She chooses to do that, and gets a comprehensive overview of where she currently stands along with suggestions for how to improve the skills which she needs to work on.

Figure 8: AI-Assisted Career Insights with SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot of the top of the Growth Portfolio app showing “Sustainable Innovation Lead” with the user’s mouse over the “Generate” button, containing the AI icon. An arrow from there points to the screenshot of the whole page below, where two columns of long text are shown under the heading “Boost your career with AI-powered insights!”. The long text has sections called “Summary”, “Suggestions for Improvement”, with sub-sections “Agile Methodology”, “Delivering High Quality Work” and “Creating and Maintaining Networks”.

The above three examples showcased the Creating Proposals as well as the Summarizing AI patterns.

AI-Assisted Performance Feedback

Emma and her colleagues appreciate getting timely and actionable feedback on their work, hearing what went well but also what could be improved. Since not every manager is a naturally talented writer, we introduced the AI writing assistant with the 1H2024 release (see my July blog post) (An example of the Creating Proposals AI pattern). This has now been improved further with the introduction of the Text Analyzer, which checks texts for any unconscious biases and other safety attributes (Figure 9). This helps all managers, because employees are far more likely to follow actionable feedback that they can relate to.

Figure 9: The new Text Analyzer in the AI writing assistant in SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: The screenshot on the left shows the drop-down of the writing assistant with the entry “Text Analyzer” in focus. A large arrow points from there to the screenshot on the right showing a popover “Use AI capabilities” with two sections: “Sentence with issue”, containing “the background noise was very annoying and distracted from what was otherwise a quality presentation”, with “very annoying” in red, and “Suggested text”, containing “the background noise was disruptive and distracted from what was otherwise a quality presentation”. The paragraph below that reads “The term “very annoying” is considered impolite and unprofessional. It can be perceived as disrespectful and may not be appropriate in a professional context”.

Another new feature are the contextual actions which are proposed in areas like spot awards and 360 review forms with some pre-defined frequently used comments across all industries – for example the option to generate a text for “Produces accurate and timely written reports”.

Emma recently received a 360-degree review, which contained a lot of lengthy and complex feedback. Fortunately, she was able to use the new AI-assisted 360-degree review capability introduced with the 2H2024 release. This gave her actionable insights and a feedback summary that highlighted her strengths and areas for improvement. Also, she received insightful comments and suggested improvements based on reviewer inputs and ratings on skills, goals, and competencies. This uses both the Summarizing and Creating Proposals AI patterns.

AI-Assisted Employee Compensation Insights

When it’s time for the salary round, the system gives managers comprehensive data on their employees’ overall compensation, that they have had to interpret themselves. But no longer! With AI assistance, SAP SuccessFactors provides compensation insights, summarizing the overall situation for a given employee and providing pertinent insights regarding compensation history, one-off payments, job progression, pay band position and more (Figure 10). As you probably guessed, this is an example of the Summarizing AI pattern.

Figure 10: AI-assisted compensation insights in SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: A screenshot of Compensation Information with a popover “Compensation Insights” containing a Summary section, with seven lines of text, followed by an “Insights” section with four numbered sub-sections containing further details: (1) Compensation History (2) One-Off Payments (3) Job Progression (4) Pay Band Position.

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this third part, covering SAP SuccessFactors – and showing concrete examples of how Generative AI embedded in business applications helps business users work more effectively, giving them unprecedented insights and convenience.

Part 4 is now available, covering latest UX innovations in SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass, i.e. applications for managing your spend (procurement) and external workforce:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 4: SAP Ariba, SAP Fieldglass.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori.
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Preventive Support for SAP’s New Era of Customer Care
entry.content:
A few years ago, mobile phones did not have functions that nowadays we consider part of our routines such as video chat and music streaming. Today’s smartphones, moreover, have evolved to include integration of functions like camera, mobile payment and various apps. Similarly, customer care approaches have developed along with cellphones. Historically, the inability of businesses to afford real-time analysis meant that customer care was mostly reactive, dealing with issues as they arose.

In an era of real-time data, this backward-looking strategy presents a challenge companies need to overcome to understand customer behavior and boost business performance. Organizations now aim to retrieve information from past, present and future to gain a comprehensive view of their processes to forecast events and identify anomalies.
